scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope progression remains tied to current treatment-plan, encounter, and follow-up evidence.
Missing assessment or treatment-plan artifacts correctly stop state progression and trigger rework.
Completion is refused when discharge or progress evidence is not present in the record of truth.
Crisis facts convert the workflow from routine operations to licensed-clinical or crisis escalation.
Disclosure without valid ROI or representative evidence is blocked and routed to the correct authority.
Part 2 uncertainty is treated as a regulated conflict instead of being flattened into standard HIPAA logic.
Conflicting tenant and public documentation rules are surfaced for escalation rather than guessed through.
Diagnosis, medication, and treatment-modality requests are routed out of the lane as licensed-clinical work.
Community mental health terms and artifacts materially change the execution logic and evidence checks.
Missing transition evidence blocks community follow-up until the required handoff records exist.
Generic service closeout is refused because the lane requires behavioral health record evidence.
Tenant-specific adaptation is allowed only with explicit mapping that preserves the public baseline.

notes: The scenario set covers normal execution, evidence gaps, crisis interruption, privacy and Part 2 handling, wrong-lane routing, generic-overgeneralization refusal, and tenant-specific adaptation.
All 10 required abilities from shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry contracts are mapped to passing scenarios.
